[2018-02-21 13:34] elyot: Canada, XXX 2018 - Lunarch Studios today announced that their much-anticipated hybrid strategy game Prismata is set for a XXX release on Steam Early Access.
 
Prismata is a sci-fi-themed turn-based strategy game that blends elements of RTS, card games, and tabletop strategy games. To win, players must build up an economy, developing technologies and honing the perfect strategy in order to create an unstoppable army.
 
Eight years in development, Prismata was designed by a team of poker players and mathematics researchers from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) who had grown frustrated with the inaccessibility of RTS games and randomness of card games.
 
Prismata narrowly achieved its Kickstarter goal of $140 000 in 2014 after an accidental deletion of the company’s mailing list resulted in a developer’s “Today I F***ed Up” story becoming one of the most upvoted Reddit posts of all time.
 
Thanks in part to a strong endorsement by professional poker player Mike "Timex" McDonald, Prismata went on to develop a community of thousands of online players who became entranced by its easy-to-play, yet difficult-to-master mechanics.
 
Now set for an Early Access release on Steam, the game launches alongside a playable story-driven campaign, with five chapters to be released monthly during the Early Access period.
[2018-02-21 13:34] elyot: ^^ Hey guys, nitpick this press release to death, go.

messages.content:
Canada, XXX 2018 - Lunarch Studios today announced that their much-anticipated hybrid strategy game Prismata is set for a XXX release on Steam Early Access.
 
Prismata is a sci-fi-themed turn-based strategy game that blends elements of RTS, card games, and tabletop strategy games. To win, players must build up an economy, developing technologies and honing the perfect strategy in order to create an unstoppable army.
 
Eight years in development, Prismata was designed by a team of poker players and mathematics researchers from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) who had grown frustrated with the inaccessibility of RTS games and randomness of card games.
 
Prismata narrowly achieved its Kickstarter goal of $140 000 in 2014 after an accidental deletion of the company’s mailing list resulted in a developer’s “Today I F***ed Up” story becoming one of the most upvoted Reddit posts of all time.
 
Thanks in part to a strong endorsement by professional poker player Mike "Timex" McDonald, Prismata went on to develop a community of thousands of online players who became entranced by its easy-to-play, yet difficult-to-master mechanics.

[2018-02-22 15:26] elyot: So F2P up front hurts in 2 ways
[2018-02-22 15:26] elyot: one is it just loses a lot of revenue that would be earned from people who would otherwise spend $25 right away
[2018-02-22 15:27] elyot: but the more important reason is that it will create a community of less devout fans who don't rate the game as highly
[2018-02-22 15:29] supergreyman (Greyman): Our concern is that it would be a huge obstacle in preventing the game from generating a lot of hype that it deserves. What is your plan to overcome that?
[2018-02-22 15:29] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): Dont you think that people that pay 25$ and then dont like it are more likely to downvote than if its f2p?
[2018-02-22 15:30] elyot: We don't think that will be a problem but we're doing a lot to target influencers etc.
[2018-02-22 15:30] elyot: Like giving away thousands and thousands of free keys
[2018-02-22 15:30] elyot: And no, not really... if it's $25 then people won't buy it unless it's a game they want, almost all steam games lose rating immediately after going F2P
[2018-02-22 15:31] elyot: just because randos get the game and downvote for troll reasons even
[2018-02-22 15:31] elyot: like if we do immediate F2P release, we would also need to remove global chat, have extensive reporting/banning, have a lot of resources to deal with ladder abuse since people could just create a ton of accounts
[2018-02-22 15:32] elyot: We're much more likely to survive if we can grow responsibly as a product than try to explode all at once and die in a fire
[2018-02-22 15:33] elyot: and if the game is genuinely good then it won't be an obstacle
[2018-02-22 15:33] elyot: if not then we can go F2P at any time and the F2P folks will all come then
[2018-02-22 15:34] supergreyman (Greyman): Do you think $25 is too much?
[2018-02-22 15:34] supergreyman (Greyman): I'm certain there will be a lot of price threads on Steam forum.
[2018-02-22 15:36] elyot: I think there are very few other products out there that give as much as we do for $25
[2018-02-22 15:36] elyot: maybe Factorio is one
[2018-02-22 15:37] elyot: They Are Billions charges $25 for much less

[2018-02-22 20:43] supergreyman (Greyman): Back at the Kickstarter, this was a very loud and clear message IMO:

*We're committed to upholding the player experience. This is our "Prismata Promise".*

*We didn't drop out of MIT to make another bullshit free-to-play game. Prismata will be different. That means:*

**No pay-to-win. Ever.** *You cannot pay for a competitive advantage in any Prismata game mode, ever. There are no packs to open.*

**No grinding.** *You'll be on a level playing field against all opponents you face. You can't gain an advantage by grinding for more rewards.*

**Genuinely free.** *No bullshit. You'll never be forced to pay to "refill your energy" or "refill your lives". We do not monetize frustration.*

**Zero regret.** *Our philosophy is that you should never regret spending money in our game. If you do, we're doing something wrong.*
[2018-02-22 20:45] supergreyman (Greyman): This is a very capturing statement, and I do hope a similar message and tone could be used in the Steam description as well.

[2018-02-22 20:52] supergreyman (Greyman): Describing how Prismata is different in bullet points and bold texts help players WAY more than paragraphs. I would fall asleep if it's in paragraph form. I like how the Kickstarter campaign does it:

**There is no other game like Prismata. At all.**

*Prismata features a number of breakthrough design decisions that completely differentiate it from other strategy games. We've stripped away a lot of the bells and whistles to distill the game down to its core strategic components, making every decision important. Prismata has:*

**No real-time pressure.** *Prismata uses turn-based gameplay with a fast timer, but your dexterity will never hold you back.*

**No decks.** *The pool of units available for purchase is the same for both players and is chosen randomly at the beginning of the game. You'll never need to pay or grind in order to be competitive.*

**No randomness.** *No unlucky draws, no tournaments decided by coinflips.*

**No fog of war.** *You'll never lose to a rock-paper-scissors "build order victory" that you were unable to predict.*

**No fixed opening book.** *Prismata's random unit pools force you to plan a new build order every game. You can't memorize your way to victory.*

**No unit-on-unit combat.** *Combat math is greatly simplified but depth is preserved.*

**No map.** *We've found a way to emphasize the core economic and strategic ideas in RTS games without the cumbersome unit positioning found in other types of turn-based games.*

*There is literally no other game out there having this combination of design choices.*

[2018-02-22 20:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): Americans need exaggeration what do u expect
[2018-02-22 20:54] crash_overlord: Also the whole thing reads way too elitist for my liking
[2018-02-22 20:54] jamesa7171: I agree with DDarkray here
[2018-02-22 20:54] crash_overlord: You want to emphasize it's new and good, not it's new and better than everything else
[2018-02-22 20:54] crash_overlord: That just offends people
[2018-02-22 20:55] crash_overlord: was that copy pastas off kickstarter
[2018-02-22 20:55] supergreyman (Greyman): Some of the wordings could be changed, but the point is that it needs to be more confident in showing off its unique features.
[2018-02-22 20:56] crash_overlord: But ye, be confident, just don't be cocky/insulting to other games imo
[2018-02-22 20:56] supergreyman (Greyman): Also, I think I understand what Prismata is about if the description is in bullet points.
[2018-02-22 20:57] supergreyman (Greyman): Far more than what a paragraph can give me.
[2018-02-22 20:57] supergreyman (Greyman): It simply clicks for me.
[2018-02-22 20:59] supergreyman (Greyman): Also, wrong channel. 😛
